title: Schema-contradiction fixture: a modern binder carrying shared_terms
summary: A structurally valid binder whose only deviation from binder-schema.json is a top-level shared_terms array. binder-schema.json (additionalProperties:false, no shared_terms property) rejects it; validate_binder.py injects the shared_terms sub-schema at check time and accepts it. The two disagree — every modern gringotts binder is invalid per karta's own published schema file.
motivation: Pin the schema-vs-validator disagreement on shared_terms so a silent divergence between the shipped schema and the runtime validator cannot pass unnoticed.
